zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Martin於1291年出生在英國英格蘭戴文可姆 馬丁時，她的父親William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin34歲，母親Eleanor FitzPiers37歲。她於1312年與Nicholas 1st Lord Audley在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1322年10月27日之前在英國英格蘭戴文。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Martin于1291年出生在英国英格兰戴文可姆 馬丁时，她的父亲William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin34岁，母亲Eleanor FitzPiers37岁。她于1312年与Nicholas 1st Lord Audley在英国英格兰斯塔福爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1322年10月27日之前在英国英格兰戴文。

nameMeanings.name: Martin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, Irish, French, Walloon, Breton, Dutch, Flemish, German, Czech, Slovak, Croatian, Italian (Veneto); Spanish (Martín): from a personal name derived from Latin Martinus, itself a derivative of Mars, genitive Martis, the Roman god of fertility and war, whose name may derive ultimately from a root mar ‘gleam’. This was borne by a famous 4th-century Christian saint, Martin of Tours, and consequently became extremely popular throughout Europe in the Middle Ages. In North America, the surname Martin has absorbed cognates and derivatives from other languages, e.g. Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Marcin , Albanian Martini , Polish surnames beginning with Marcin-, and Slovenian patronymics like Martinčič (see Martincic ). Martin is the most frequent surname in France and one of the most frequent surnames in Wallonia.
English: variant of   Marton  .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Mártain, ‘descendant of Martin’ (compare 1 above). Otherwise, a shortened form of Gilmartin or McMartin ; sometimes also spelled Martyn.

variants.name: Marczewski
meanings: Polish: habitational name for someone from Marcewo (formerly also Marczewo) in Greater Poland Voivodeship. Alternatively, a derivative of the personal name Marc, Marcz, a pet form of Marcin, Marek (see Martin , Mark ).

variants.name: Marciniak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Eugeniusz, Ewa, Agnieszka, Bartosz, Boleslaw, Ignatius, Jaroslaw, Jerzy, Krystyna, Ryszard, Slawomir, Stanislawa.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marak
meanings: Czech:
(Marák): from a pet form of the personal names Martin or Marek .
(Mařák): metronymic from the female personal name Máří, Czech form of Maria . It may also denoted the husband of a woman named Máří.

variants.name: Machain
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Matxain, a topographic or habitational name referring to a house named with a diminutive of the personal name Martin .

variants.name: Maertens
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from the personal name Maerten, a variant of Martin .
North German (Märtens): see Martens .

variants.name: Marcin
meanings: Slovak, Rusyn (from Slovakia), and Polish: from Marcin, a personal name equivalent to Martin . In Poland this is the most usual form as a personal name, but the surname is very rare there, while in Slovakia, where Marcin is a dialect form of the name Martin, the situation is opposite.
American shortened form of Polish Marcinkiewicz and other derivatives of the personal name Marcin (see 1 above).
In some cases possibly also French: from a vernacular form of the personal name Marcien, derived from the Latin surname Marcianus, popularized through the cult of a local saint.

variants.name: Macho
meanings: Spanish: nickname from macho ‘male; mule’ (from Latin masculus ‘male, masculine’), applied either to denote a virile, strong man or alternatively an idiot.
Spanish: possibly also a nickname for a forceful person or a metonymic occupational name for a smith, from macho ‘sledgehammer’ (from Latin marculus ‘hammer’).
Slovak: from a derivative of the personal name   Martin  .

variants.name: Marcinowski
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Aleksander, Bogdan, Jerzy, Zbigniew, Zigmund.
Polish: habitational name for someone from any of the places called Marcinów, Marcinowa, Marcinowice, or any of numerous other places in Poland named with the personal name Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Marcinkiewicz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Henryk, Beata, Elzbieta, Janina.
Polish: patronymic from the personal name Marcinek, a petform of Marcin (see Martin ).

variants.name: Mar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Gim, Wai, Fong, Hong, Man Chung, Moo, Mun, Quon, Wing, Yat, Bok, Chun.
Spanish, Portuguese, Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Catalan: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mar ‘sea’ (from Latin mare).
German: nickname from Middle High German mar(w) ‘tender, delicate’, or an altered form of Mahr, a topographic name from mar ‘lake, swamp, stagnant water’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Martin was born in 1291, in Combe Martin, Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin, was 34 and her mother, Eleanor FitzPiers, was 37. She married Nicholas 1st Lord Audley in 1312, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died before 27 October 1322, in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Martin est née en 1291, à Combe Martin, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Eleanor FitzPiers, avait 37 ans. Elle a épousé Nicholas 1st Lord Audley en 1312, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée avant 27 octobre 1322, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Martin 1291 in Combe Martin, Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin 34 und ihre Mutter Eleanor FitzPiers 37. Sie hat 1312 in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Nicholas 1st Lord Audley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb vor 27. Oktober 1322 in Devon, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Martin è nata il nel 1291 nel Combe Martin, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Eleanor FitzPiers, aveva 37 anni. Lei sposato Nicholas 1st Lord Audley nel 1312, nel Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì prima del 27 ottobre 1322, nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Martinが1291年にイギリスイギリスデボンコンベ マルティンで生まれた時，父William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martinは34歳，母Eleanor FitzPiersは37歳であった。1312年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアでNicholas 1st Lord Audleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1322年10月27日以前にイギリスイギリスデボンで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Martin(이)가 1291년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 코브 마틴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor FitzPiers(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 1312년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르에서 Nicholas 1st Lord Audley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1322년 10월 27일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Martin nasceu em 1291, em Combe Martin, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor FitzPiers, tinha 37 anos. Ela casou-se com Nicholas 1st Lord Audley em 1312, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu antes 27 de outubro de 1322, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Martin родилась в 1291 г. в местности комб мартинь, девон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor FitzPiers, 37. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas 1st Lord Audley в 1312 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла перед 27 октябрь 1322 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Martin nació en 1291, en Combe Martin, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Martin 1st Baron of Combe Martin, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Eleanor FitzPiers, tenía 37 años.
